summary: Implementation covers the main no-auth/Basic REST and MCP paths, but REST OAuth tool calls can execute the endpoint runtime twice before returning. Because delay is now applied inside callPermittedEndpointByName, a permitted OAuth REST call can incur the configured delay twice, and a 30000 ms timeout shortcut can become roughly a 60000 ms call. That contradicts the reliability/spec requirement that artificial delays are bounded to 30000 ms per configured endpoint call. The required checks passed, but they do not cover this OAuth REST delay path.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Delay behavior is implemented and tested for no-auth REST and MCP calls.
Forced REST/MCP tool and protocol error mapping is implemented and covered by unit tests plus @failure-delay-error E2E.
Timeout shortcut is visible in the endpoint UI and persisted through the API without running a long E2E wait.
Required deterministic commands in the provided summary passed: npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e -- --grep @failure-delay-error, and npm run verify.
The task maps to docs/product-specs/failure-simulation-and-audit.md and does not appear to include malformed response work.
missing_requirements: REST OAuth bearer tool calls need to avoid executing callPermittedEndpointByName twice now that endpoint calls can sleep. The current lib/rest/http.ts pre-check can add an extra configured delay before the actual body is parsed and executed.
Add or adjust coverage for delayed REST OAuth calls, or refactor the permission/not-found pre-check so it does not execute runtime behavior before the real call.
human_review_triggers: Required checks do not prove the claimed behavior across REST auth modes.
Implementation changes currently contradict docs/RELIABILITY.md delay bound semantics for OAuth REST calls.
